# Contributing to DBFlow Labs

We value engineering quality over quantity.

Before submitting any code:

1. Run the existing test suites in the target repository and confirm all tests pass.
2. Ensure 100% test coverage for new features and modified code paths.
3. Follow project naming conventions and PSR standards.
4. Update documentation for any public API, configuration, or behavioral change.

## Pull Requests

- Keep changes focused and scoped to a single concern.
- Provide a clear summary, testing evidence, and architectural context in the PR description.
- Do not submit PRs without corresponding tests unless explicitly agreed upon in an issue.

## Issues

- Open bug reports only for confirmed, reproducible defects.
- For usage questions, consult repository documentation and the official site at [https://dbflow.dev](https://dbflow.dev) before filing an issue.
- Feature requests must include problem statement, proposed solution, and architectural impact assessment.

## Code Review Standards

All contributions are reviewed against deterministic integrity, concurrency safety, strict typing, and maintainability. Submissions that increase complexity without measurable benefit will be rejected.
